answer: On September 15, 2026 the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline deploy introduced a bad join key (normalized_tax_id) replacing the correct join key (closing_party_role_id) when joining settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge. Because the same tax identifier can appear in multiple roles (buyer, seller, trust, entity) within a closing, the join fan-out inflated reconciliation totals, overstating escrow disbursement reconciliation variance for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title. The impacted view was finance.disbursement_recon_daily. Remediation: revert the join to use closing_party_role_id.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Revert the join to use closing_party_role_id instead of normalized_tax_id
facts.root_cause: The September 15 deploy of settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 introduced a join from settlement.escrow_disbursement_fact to party.party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id, causing fan-out because the same tax identifier can appear in multiple roles within a closing
